title: Browser-E2E executor contract (P-C — executor MECHANICS)
description: The HOW of a browser-E2E evidence run: app start / readiness / shutdown, base URL, allowed LOCAL origins, and the declared user journeys. Owned by Deliver/adopter, MUTABLE — these MECHANICS PRODUCE evidence; they NEVER define pass/fail (the signed functional-checklist criteria do, and Acceptance judges). v1 is local/demo oriented: no auth, no remote deploy, no cloud browser. The DRIVER injects per-run values at the e2e gate: it substitutes the literal tokens {port}/{store}/{mode} in app_start_cmd, sets the concrete base_url (host from the static base_url + the allocated port), and provides PORT/STORE/MODE to the child env — so the static charter form stays portless/templated and the runtime form is concrete. This contract is the shape of charter.tooling.e2e (the charter validator cross-checks it; the driver re-validates the runtime form).

executor_contract_id.description: Stable id; part of the M3 calibration identity (a change invalidates the M3 record).

executor_kind.description: local_http = deterministic offline stdlib executor (CI). playwright = real browser, opt-in + env-gated (AIDAZI_E2E_PLAYWRIGHT=1); never run in offline CI.

app_start_cmd.description: Command that starts the app under test. Array (argv) preferred; a string is wrapped as a single-element argv. The driver substitutes the literal tokens {port}, {store}, {mode} at runtime; a real app uses whatever flags it needs (the tokens are optional).

url.description: Path (relative to base_url) polled for HTTP 200 (e.g. /__health).

cmd.description: Alternative: a shell probe that must exit 0.

base_url.description: Application URL. Local, staging, and production are supported; its origin MUST be explicitly listed in allowed_origins.

process_owned.description: true ⇒ the orchestrator terminates the process it started (the default behavior).

allowed_origins.description: Explicit origins Acceptance may navigate, including production origins when authorized.

env.description: Extra env for the app process. Put env-var NAMES the adopter sets out-of-band; do NOT inline secret values.

storage_state.description: Optional Playwright storage-state file path; secrets remain out of the charter.

channel.description: Optional installed browser channel, e.g. chrome.

executable_path.description: Optional installed browser executable used instead of a downloaded Playwright browser.

store.description: Backend store path the app persists to (carried to the child via STORE env). The driver sets a per-run temp path.

mode.description: App-mode selector carried to the child via MODE env (a fixture/demo convenience; real apps omit it).

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the app process (e.g. so a module is importable).

port.description: Optional explicit port when base_url has none; usually the driver allocates a free port and writes it into base_url.

evidence_retention_path.description: Optional override of the evidence root; default .orchestrator/audit/browser.

id.description: Optional step label (names the screenshot snapshot).

url.description: navigate target; or for assert_request_ok the URL substring to match in the captured network log.

criterion_id.description: Links an assertion step to a functional-checklist criterion_id (so its per-criterion executor_status is produced). MUST exist in the signed checklist (the executor fail-closes on a mismatch).

critical.description: true ⇒ a CAPTURED failure on this step is a critical veto (a milestone pass becomes needs_human, §3.2). A blocking RUNTIME failure (step cannot execute) → gate_hard_fail regardless of this flag.

side_effect.description: Named production side effect governed by the Acceptance production policy.
